question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a network identifier that causes getBucketName() to resolve to an attacker-controlled cloud bucket via ImporterProperties.HederaNetwork.getBlockStreamBucketName(), allowing the importer to ingest attacker-supplied block files and reorganize the persisted transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fallback logic in getBucketName() — where a blank bucketName causes the bucket to be derived from the network name — to redirect block file downloads to a bucket they control by poisoning the network-to-bucket mapping, causing the importer to process fabricated blocks and reorder transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user, by injecting a malicious entry into the nodes collection (e.g., via a publicly writable configuration endpoint or environment variable injection), cause BlockNodeSubscriber to stream blocks from an attacker-controlled gRPC server, enabling the attacker to serve reordered or fabricated block sequences that alter the mirror node's transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isAutoDiscoveryEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the autoDiscoveryEnabled flag — which causes BlockNodeDiscoveryService to pull block node endpoints from the registered node database — by submitting a RegisteredNode transaction on-chain that advertises a malicious block node endpoint, causing the importer to stream blocks from the attacker's server and reorganize transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSourceType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AUTO sourceType in BlockProperties, where CompositeBlockSource switches from BLOCK_NODE to FILE source after 3 consecutive errors, by deliberately causing 3 gRPC errors on the legitimate block node to force a fallback to a cloud bucket the attacker has poisoned with reordered block files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the configurable frequency (default 100ms) of the scheduled block polling in CompositeBlockSource to cause a race condition between two block sources during cutover, where blocks from different sources are interleaved, resulting in a reordered transaction history in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getLedger()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the LedgerProperties configuration in BlockProperties — specifically the historyProofVerificationKey and ledgerId fields — by supplying a crafted ledger configuration that causes TssVerifierImpl.verify() to accept forged TSS signatures on attacker-crafted blocks, enabling transaction history reorganization?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getLedger()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the fallback path in TssVerifierImpl.getLedger() — where blockProperties.getLedger() is used when no ledger exists in the database — by ensuring the ledger table is empty and the LedgerProperties in BlockProperties contains attacker-controlled keys, bypassing TSS signature verification and allowing fabricated blocks to be accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxBlockItems limit in StreamProperties (default 800,000) by sending a block stream response that approaches but does not exceed the limit, causing the BlockAssembler to accept an oversized block containing reordered or duplicated transactions that alter the mirror node's view of transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxStreamResponseSize (default 36MB) in StreamProperties to craft a block stream response that is exactly at the size boundary, causing partial block parsing in BlockNode.streamBlocks() that results in an incomplete or reordered set of transactions being committed to the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxSubscribeAttempts (default 3) in StreamProperties to force BlockNode.onError() to mark a legitimate block node as inactive, causing BlockNodeSubscriber to fall back to a lower-priority or attacker-controlled node that serves reordered blocks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readmitDelay (default 1 minute) in StreamProperties — by repeatedly triggering errors on the primary block node — to keep it inactive long enough for the importer to consume a full sequence of attacker-crafted blocks from a secondary node, reorganizing transaction history before the primary node is readmitted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStream()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the responseTimeout (default 400ms) in StreamProperties to cause BlockNode.timeout() to expire mid-block assembly, leaving the BlockAssembler in a partial state, and then trigger a reconnect that causes the same block to be processed twice or out of order, reorganizing transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the enabled=false default in BlockProperties, combined with the CutoverServiceImpl.isCutoverComplete() logic that automatically sets enabled=true when cutover completes, to trigger a premature cutover by manipulating the version field of the last record file in the database, causing the importer to switch to block stream processing before the network is ready?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the persistBytes=false default in BlockProperties — which causes AbstractBlockSource.onBlockStream() to null out the raw bytes of processed block files — to prevent forensic detection of block manipulation, allowing a history reorganization attack to go undetected because the original block bytes are not retained for audit?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the writeFiles=false default in BlockProperties to prevent block files from being archived to the stream path, eliminating the audit trail that would otherwise allow detection of a transaction history reorganization attack?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that nodes defaults to an empty collection in BlockProperties, causing BlockNodeDiscoveryService.getBlockNodes() to rely entirely on auto-discovered nodes from the database, and then inject a malicious block node via an on-chain RegisteredNode transaction to become the sole block source and serve reordered transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSourceType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BlockSourceType.AUTO mode in BlockProperties, where CompositeBlockSource.getSourceHealth() switches to FILE source when no block nodes are discovered, by ensuring the auto-discovery cache returns empty (e.g., by triggering a RegisteredNodeChangedEvent that clears the cache) and simultaneously poisoning the cloud bucket with reordered block files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StringUtils.isNotBlank() check in getBucketName() by supplying a bucketName consisting entirely of whitespace characters, causing the method to fall through to the network-derived bucket name, which the attacker has pre-registered as a malicious bucket containing reordered block files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the minimum frequency constraint (NotNull but no minimum enforced) in BlockProperties to cause extremely rapid polling that creates a race condition in CutoverServiceImpl.get(), where the synchronized block is bypassed due to thread scheduling, allowing two different stream types to process blocks simultaneously and produce a reordered transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getLedger()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nodeContributions list in LedgerProperties — which is used by TssVerifierImpl.onLedgerSet() to call TSS.setAddressBook() — by supplying a crafted list with manipulated weights that lowers the threshold for TSS signature acceptance, allowing a minority of colluding nodes to produce a valid signature on a fabricated block that reorganizes transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getLedger()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of validation on the historyProofVerificationKey bytes in LedgerProperties to supply a zero-length or all-zero key that causes WRAPSVerificationKey.setCurrentKey() in TssVerifierImpl.onLedgerSet() to accept any signature, enabling the attacker to serve blocks with forged TSS signatures that reorganize transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getLedger()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ledgerId field in LedgerProperties — used directly in TSS.verifyTSS(ledgerId, signature, message) — by supplying a crafted ledgerId that matches an attacker-controlled TSS key pair, allowing the attacker to generate valid signatures for fabricated blocks that reorganize transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the priority field in BlockNodeProperties (default 0, minimum 0) to ensure an attacker-controlled block node is selected first by BlockNodeSubscriber.getNode(), by registering a node with priority 0 (highest) that serves reordered blocks before legitimate nodes can respond?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getMergeKey() logic in BlockNodeProperties — which uses endpoint+requiresTls as the merge key — to override a legitimate block node configuration in BlockNodeDiscoveryService.getBlockNodes() by registering a discovered node with the same host:port but different priority, causing the attacker's node configuration to replace the legitimate one?
